title: Project Report: CodeQuest Teacher Review Platform — Full Stack Web Developer
author: Gagan Pasupuleti
summary: Teachers log in, browse reports by family, mark rubric scores, download PDF — full auth stack.
source_file: CodeQuest project specification (official docs + industry patterns)

chapters.title: Project Overview
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Project Overview** *(Level: Intermediate)*

**CodeQuest Teacher Review Platform** is a intermediate-level end-to-end project for Full Stack Web Developer. Duration: 4–5 weeks. Teachers log in, browse reports by family, mark rubric scores, download PDF — full auth stack. Tools: React, Express, PostgreSQL, JWT.

Code Reference:
```text
Objective: JWT auth with bcrypt passwords
Objective: Role-based access (teacher/student)
Objective: Report listing from catalog.json
Objective: Marking form with rubric fields
```
What it shows: Lists measurable outcomes before you start building.

### What it actually is
A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.

### When to use it
At project kickoff.

### Where to use it
Full Stack Web Developer portfolios and CodeQuest project submissions.

### Real use example
A candidate lists this project on a Full Stack Web Developer resume with a demo link.
key_takeaways: A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.
A candidate lists this project on a Full Stack Web Developer resume with a demo link.

chapters.title: Phase: Auth
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Auth** *(Level: Intermediate)*

Register/login API. Complete these tasks: bcrypt hash; JWT middleware. Deliverable for this phase: Auth Postman tests.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] bcrypt hash
- [ ] JWT middleware
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### What it actually is
Phase Auth is one step in the full project lifecycle.

### When to use it
During week 1 of the project timeline.

### Where to use it
Full Stack Web Developer agile sprints and coursework milestones.

### Real use example
Team demo shows the Auth Postman tests to the teacher.

chapters.title: Phase: Deploy

**Chapter focus: Phase: Deploy** *(Level: Intermediate)*

```text
- [ ] PostgreSQL on Supabase
- [ ] CI pipeline
```

chapters.title: Final Deliverables and Review
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Final Deliverables and Review** *(Level: Intermediate)*

Submit all final deliverables: Production app; API docs; User guide; Demo video. Skills demonstrated: Full stack, Auth, CRUD. Prepare a 5-minute walkthrough video and README for CodeQuest teacher marking.

Code Reference:
```text
Deliverable: Production app
Deliverable: API docs
Deliverable: User guide
Deliverable: Demo video
```
What it shows: Final checklist ensures nothing is missing at submission.

### What it actually is
Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.

### When to use it
Before deadline and teacher review.

### Where to use it
CodeQuest project gallery and interviews.

### Real use example
A student submits Production app and passes the rubric.
